transcript.clean_text: beautiful weather on stage nine of love welter reminiscent of the sunspot which had illuminated jay vines second stage win of this race finishing fifth behind him ever nepal head of bass and roglic the three amigos at the top of the overall classification blanketed by a minute and one how would ever nepal's team cope in the absence of peter serry who left the race due to covet we're going to find out j vine in the polkadot jersey looking very happy after two stage wins would you try and make it three of course you would simon yates would won this stage back in 2018 how would he go on the ramps that defined the day and what ramps they are well rolling terrain before the mountains began and we cut back for an intermediate sprint near our start town and a peak at the end which defied belief 24 in places all squeezed in to three and a half kilometers of hell at the end of the day well they set off 176 kilometers ahead of them and it was a spirited battle to be in the break final incarnation of it saw 10 up the road but dsm's aaronsbone was three minutes and 18 down on the overall and his team thought that maybe it should be another day for him well they may well rue that decision because the breakaway was so strong it had legs and the gap was up over five minutes at one time but always moderated by quick step alpha vinyl working of course for evernote well towards the end of the day and that climb that everyone dreaded it was defining itself even before we got there the breakaway had split ballistella was up front arguing with janssen's about work rate and a crash in the peloton meant that there were many gaps that opened up and nerves started to fray this was going to play into the hands of the break they may well have been fracturing but louis menkes looked absolutely magnificent for intermarching wanti equally magnificent the world champion junior philippe coming down one side of this v-shaped valley that defined the finale he making sure everyone stayed safe and indeed made sure that remko evan nepal was perfectly poised to attack which of course he did as menkes was making ground up the mountain evan nepal was making hay compared to those around him in the general classification he looked absolutely supreme comfortable you might even say likewise louis menkes who's pushing on for glory but what about this man evan nepal with enric madison looked back there he could see of course primos roglic struggling and one a user of ue team emirates also being bitten by the pace that he was setting this was absolutely magnificent he was going to find more time on those surrounding him in the general classification the biggest loser i think primos roglic really struggling henrik mass likewise was finding life more than a little bit hard louis menkes while he was heading for a stage win never had one on the grand tour he won the copy bartoli when he was a youngster back in 2015. now at the age of 30 he came to the top of the hill to get his first ever grand tour podium it was a wonderful moment louis menkes with a stage win in a grand tour it's a pleasure to say and this was a pleasure to see remco having a pop crossing the line and the clock started 44 seconds he had as an advantage in the end of a mass and a minute and 12 added to his advantage over primos rodriguez uzo did not want to miss out on a good finish either plenty were just glad to see the line they'll be glad to see the rest day as well roglich home bound perhaps he will be back what more for louis menkes but he's just going to enjoy this for the time being he'll enjoy the rest day as well not so the time trial i don't think and it's a time trial to come this is how it finished today evan apollo remarkable fourth place simon yates down in 12th two minutes 37 off the winning time pandon suffering today megalanga lopez valverde around him as well everyone was tortured and this man well he was the man turning the screw ever nepal leads the race now by a minute and 12 over enric mass a minute and 53 over primus roglich and with a time trial to come after the rest day he will have no fear so rest day and then on tuesday a resumption of hostilities 31.1 kilometers is the time trial run to alicante a race of truth against the clock on virtually pan-flat terrain it will suit ever nepal but who else can make inroads into his lead join us and find out at you
